title: angelica sylvestris var. villosa
intro: Angelica sylvestris var. villosa, also known as Hairy Angelica or Wild Angelica, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant is also valued for its ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate menstrual cramps, among other health benefits. Herbal preparations of Angelica sylvestris var. villosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
